perturbation.original_text: FCC agrees to provide $5,000,000 of commercial liability insurance in support of this indemnity which names Flotek as additional insured, with waiver of subrogation.
perturbation.changed_text: 25. Paragraph Headings. The paragraph headings used herein are descriptive only and shall have no legal force or effect whatsoever. 26. Gender. Whenever the context so requires, the masculine shall include the feminine and neuter, and the singular shall include the plural and conversely. FCC agrees to provide $5,000,000 of commercial liability insurance in support of this indemnity which names Flotek as additional insured, with waiver of subrogation.
perturbation.explanation: The insurance clause requiring 5M liability and naming Flotek as additional insured has been moved to the section discussing paragraph headings and gender definitions. This obscures the clause within irrelevant content, making it less likely to be noticed or enforced. It no longer resides within the 'Personal Injury and Property Damage Liability Indemnification' section, where it would naturally be expected.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Delaware law regarding insurance requirements in contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law requires clear and unambiguous language regarding insurance coverage and indemnification in contracts to ensure both parties are aware of their obligations and rights. By burying the insurance requirement clause within a section unrelated to liability or indemnification, the modification obfuscates a key contractual obligation and increases the ambiguity around the enforceability of the clause, potentially violating the spirit, if not the letter, of Delaware contract law concerning clarity and transparency in insurance-related provisions.

(a) All bonds, specialties, and notes in writing, payable to any person, or order, or assigns, may be assigned, or indorsed, and the assignee, or indorsee, his or her executors, administrators, or assigns, may again assign, or indorse the same, as often as desired. The assignees, or indorsees, or their executors, or administrators, may, in their own name, sue for and recover the money due on the bonds, specialties, or notes. All assignments of bonds or specialties shall be under hand and seal, and executed before at least one credible witness. (b) Assignors, or indorsers, or their executors, or administrators may not release or discharge any sum due by the bonds, specialties, or notes, after the date of the assignment, and no release, receipt, or discharge from him, her or them made after the date of the assignment shall be good or available. perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a structural flaw by moving the $5,000,000 commercial liability insurance clause to a section on paragraph headings and gender definitions. This action obscures the clause, which would normally be in the 'Personal Injury and Property Damage Liability Indemnification' section, making it less noticeable and potentially harder to enforce. The Law Explanation supports this, noting that Delaware law requires clear and unambiguous language regarding insurance coverage. By burying the insurance requirement, the modification obfuscates a key contractual obligation and increases ambiguity, which the Law Explanation says violates Delaware contract law concerning clarity and transparency in insurance-related provisions. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to Title 6, Chapter 27 of the Delaware Code, which generally covers contracts, reinforcing that contracts in Delaware are subject to legal scrutiny.

perturbation.original_text: Any waiver by either party to be enforceable must be in writing and no waiver by either party shall constitute a continuing waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Purchase and Sale. Flotek will from time to time during the Term purchase from FCC, and FCC will sell to Flotek, Terpene Product. 3. Term. The "Term" shall begin on the date hereof and shall expire December 31, 2023, subject to early termination pursuant to the terms of Section 19. Flotek, may, by written notice given to FCC on or before September 30, 2023, elect for the Term to be extended to December 31, 2024. Any waiver by either party to be enforceable must be in writing and no waiver by either party shall constitute a continuing waiver.
perturbation.explanation: The waiver clause, which dictates how contractual rights can be relinquished, has been moved to the early sections defining Purchase and Sale and Term. This makes it less likely to be reviewed in conjunction with sections on breach or remedies, where its relevance would be more apparent. Now, the parties involved may not understand when the rights in this contract cannot be waived.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law on Waiver Requirements
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement (Second) of Contracts emphasizes the importance of understanding the context and implications of a waiver. By placing the waiver clause in an unrelated section, it disconnects the clause from the sections detailing the actual rights and obligations that could be waived, making it more difficult to understand the scope and effect of any potential waiver, which is not supported by contract law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: waiver | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you waiver A waiver is the intentional or voluntary relinquishment or surrender of a recognized right or privilege . In legal contexts, a waiver is often documented in writing, but it can also be implied through actions . By waiving a right, an individual or party chooses not to enforce a particular right, claim , or provision they are legally entitled to. [Last reviewed in July of 2024 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex accidents and injuries COMMERCE commercial activities LIFE EVENTS accidents & injuries (tort law) THE LEGAL PROCESS courts criminal procedure business law contracts wex definitions business sectors civil procedure individual rights legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification creates a structural flaw by relocating the waiver clause, potentially obscuring its significance and applicability within the contract. The Wex definition of 'waiver' states it's 'the intentional or voluntary relinquishment or surrender of a recognized right or privilege'. By moving the waiver clause to a less relevant section, the parties might not fully understand which rights they are relinquishing or when these rights cannot be waived, thus affecting the enforceability and understanding of the waiver, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: FCC warrants to Flotek that: (a) all of the Terpene Product supplied by FCC to Flotek shall: (i) conform to the specifications set forth in Section 1; 3 (ii) comply with all relevant laws and regulations including, without limitation, laws and regulations of each of the jurisdictions in which the Terpene Product are either manufactured or to be sold or used concerning purity, sanitation, safety, security, and packaging and labeling of food and beverage; (iii) be in good condition at the time of delivery in all respects; and (iv) be free from any defect in design, workmanship, materials and packaging; and (b) it shall convey to Flotek good title to the Terpene Product free of any encumbrance, lien or security interest;
perturbation.changed_text: 27. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of Delaware. 4 28. No Presumption Against Any Party. Neither this Agreement nor any uncertainty or ambiguity in this Agreement shall be construed or resolved against any party, whether under any rule of construction or otherwise. On the contrary, this Agreement has been reviewed by each of the parties and their counsel (or the party has elected not to consult with counsel of its own choosing) and shall be construed and interpreted according to the ordinary meaning of the words used so as to fairly accomplish the purposes and intentions of all parties. FCC warrants to Flotek that: (a) all of the Terpene Product supplied by FCC to Flotek shall: (i) conform to the specifications set forth in Section 1; 3 (ii) comply with all relevant laws and regulations including, without limitation, laws and regulations of each of the jurisdictions in which the Terpene Product are either manufactured or to be sold or used concerning purity, sanitation, safety, security, and packaging and labeling of food and beverage; (iii) be in good condition at the time of delivery in all respects; and (iv) be free from any defect in design, workmanship, materials and packaging; and (b) it shall convey to Flotek good title to the Terpene Product free of any encumbrance, lien or security interest;
perturbation.explanation: The warranty clause has been moved to the general provisions, specifically the governing law section. This relocation significantly diminishes its prominence, as warranties are typically expected within the section detailing product specifications, liabilities, or product acceptance. Moving it buries it and reduces the buyer's awareness of their protection and legal rights.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-313 requires that express warranties are clearly stated and made part of the basis of the bargain. Moving the express warranty to an unexpected location (governing law section) undermines its prominence and makes it less likely to be considered a basis of the bargain, thus reducing its enforceability and contradicting the intent of the UCC.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-313. Express Warranties by Affirmation, Promise, Description, Sample. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-313. Express Warranties by Affirmation, Promise, Description, Sample. (1) Express warranties by the seller are created as follows: (a) Any affirmation of fact or promise made by the seller to the buyer which relates to the goods and becomes part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the goods shall conform to the affirmation or promise. (b) Any description of the goods which is made part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the goods shall conform to the description. (c) Any sample or model which is made part of the basis of the bargain creates an express warranty that the whole of the goods shall conform to the sample or model. (2) It is not necessary to the creation of an express warranty that the seller use formal words such as "warrant" or "guarantee" or that he have a specific intention to make a warranty, but an affirmation merely of the value of the goods or a statement purporting to be merely the seller's opinion or commendation of the goods does not create a warranty. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The relocation of the warranty clause from a prominent position to the general provisions section, specifically the governing law section, creates a structural flaw that contradicts UCC § 2-313. The law snippet states that express warranties become part of the basis of the bargain. By burying the warranty in an unexpected location, the changed text undermines its prominence and makes it less likely to be considered part of the basis of the bargain. This reduces its enforceability and contradicts the intent of the UCC, as warranties are typically expected within the section detailing product specifications, liabilities, or product acceptance, making this relocation a structural flaw that diminishes the buyer's awareness of their protection and legal rights. Therefore, a contradiction exists because the modified contract weakens the warranty's enforceability by obscuring its presence.
